data.title: CrossFit MINS Workout
data.text: Workout of the Day 26.10.2025 WODDAILYSWEAT For Quality 6 Rounds 3:00 Row/Ski Erg 20 Box Stepups 15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16) 10 Ring Rows Sweat: 16/8 Train: 20/12 Compete: 24/16"
data.full_text:
Workout of the Day

26.10.2025

WOD
DAILYSWEAT


For Quality
6 Rounds
3:00 Row/Ski Erg
20 Box Stepups
15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16)
10 Ring Rows


Sweat: 16/8
Train: 20/12
Compete: 24/16"

ai_explanation.explanation:
Workout Overview


Hey everyone! Today’s WOD is all about quality and control. We’re going to be moving through six rounds of four different movements, focusing on maintaining good form and a steady pace. This workout will challenge your cardiovascular endurance, lower body strength, and upper body pulling power. Remember, the goal here is to perform each movement with precision rather than rushing through the rounds. Let’s break it down!


Workout Components


1. Row/Ski Erg (3:00)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • For the Row: Sit on the rower with your feet secured. Grab the handle with both hands, keeping your arms straight. Start with your knees bent and your back straight.
  • Push through your legs, lean back slightly, and pull the handle to your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body. Reverse the motion to return to the starting position.
  • For the Ski Erg: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Grip the handles with both hands. Start with your arms overhead and pull down in a smooth motion, engaging your core and legs as you drive down.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the movement.
  • For the row, focus on driving through your legs first before pulling with your arms.
  • For the Ski Erg, use your legs to assist your arms in the pull.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • If you’re new to rowing or skiing, consider reducing the time to 2:00 or even 1:30 to build confidence.
  • You can also switch to a stationary bike if available.

    • 2. Box Step-Ups (20 reps)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • Stand facing the box with your feet hip-width apart. Step up onto the box with one foot, driving through your heel to lift your body up.
  • Bring your other foot up to meet the first on the box, then step back down one foot at a time.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your chest up and engage your core as you step up.
  • Make sure your entire foot is on the box to avoid any missteps.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • Use a lower box if you’re not comfortable with the height.
  • You can also perform step-ups without weights or use a dumbbell for added resistance.

    • 3. Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (15 reps)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed out. Place the kettlebell between your feet.
  • Hinge at your hips to grab the kettlebell with both hands. Keep your back straight and chest up.
  • Drive through your heels to stand up, pulling the kettlebell up towards your chin, keeping your elbows high.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Focus on using your legs to lift the kettlebell rather than just your arms.
  • Keep the kettlebell close to your body throughout the movement.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • Use a lighter kettlebell if you’re new to this movement.
  • If kettlebells are not available, you can substitute with dumbbell high pulls.

    • 4. Ring Rows (10 reps)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • Set the rings at a height that allows you to lean back with your arms extended. Grab the rings with both hands and walk your feet forward until your body is at an angle.
  • Pull your chest towards the rings by bending your elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together. Lower yourself back down with control.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your body straight from head to heels; avoid sagging your hips.
  • Engage your core throughout the movement to maintain stability.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • If rings are too challenging, you can perform inverted rows using a barbell or a sturdy table.
  • Alternatively, you can do banded rows with a resistance band.

    • Loading and Rep-Modification Options


  • **Sweat:**
  • Row/Ski: 2:00
  • Box Step-Ups: 16”/8” box
  • Kettlebell: 16kg/8kg
  • Ring Rows: 8 reps

  • **Train:**
  • Row/Ski: 3:00
  • Box Step-Ups: 20”/12” box
  • Kettlebell: 20kg/12kg
  • Ring Rows: 10 reps

  • **Compete:**
  • Row/Ski: 3:00
  • Box Step-Ups: 24”/16” box
  • Kettlebell: 24kg/16kg
  • Ring Rows: 12 reps

    • Strategy Advice

  • Focus on maintaining a steady pace throughout the workout. It’s better to keep moving consistently than to sprint and burn out.
  • Break up the box step-ups and kettlebell high pulls into manageable sets if needed, especially if you start to feel fatigued.
  • Stay mindful of your form; quality over quantity is key today!

    • Alright team, let’s get after it! Remember to hydrate, listen to your body, and have fun!

combined_content.title: CrossFit MINS Workout

WOD:


For Quality

6 Rounds:

  • 3:00 Row/Ski Erg
  • 20 Box Step-ups
  • 15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16)
  • 10 Ring Rows

    • Sweat: 16/8

    Train: 20/12

    Compete: 24/16

We’re going to be moving through six rounds of four different movements, focusing on maintaining good form and a steady pace. This workout will challenge your cardiovascular endurance, lower body strength, and upper body pulling power. Remember, the goal here is to perform each movement with precision rather than rushing through the rounds. Let’s break it down! ## Workout Components ### 1. Row/Ski Erg (3:00) - **Technique Steps:** - For the Row: Sit on the rower with your feet secured. Grab the handle with both hands, keeping your arms straight. Start with your knees bent and your back straight. - Push through your legs, lean back slightly, and pull the handle to your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body. Reverse the motion to return to the starting position. - For the Ski Erg: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Grip the handles with both hands. Start with your arms overhead and pull down in a smooth motion, engaging your core and legs as you drive down. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the movement. - For the row, focus on driving through your legs first before pulling with your arms. - For the Ski Erg, use your legs to assist your arms in the pull. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - If you’re new to rowing or skiing, consider reducing the time to 2:00 or even 1:30 to build confidence. - You can also switch to a stationary bike if available. ### 2. Box Step-Ups (20 reps) - **Technique Steps:** - Stand facing the box with your feet hip-width apart. Step up onto the box with one foot, driving through your heel to lift your body up. - Bring your other foot up to meet the first on the box, then step back down one foot at a time. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your chest up and engage your core as you step up. - Make sure your entire foot is on the box to avoid any missteps. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - Use a lower box if you’re not comfortable with the height. - You can also perform step-ups without weights or use a dumbbell for added resistance. ### 3. Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (15 reps) - **Technique Steps:** - Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed out. Place the kettlebell between your feet. - Hinge at your hips to grab the kettlebell with both hands. Keep your back straight and chest up. - Drive through your heels to stand up, pulling the kettlebell up towards your chin, keeping your elbows high. - **Form Tips:** - Focus on using your legs to lift the kettlebell rather than just your arms. - Keep the kettlebell close to your body throughout the movement. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - Use a lighter kettlebell if you’re new to this movement. - If kettlebells are not available, you can substitute with dumbbell high pulls. ### 4. Ring Rows (10 reps) - **Technique Steps:** - Set the rings at a height that allows you to lean back with your arms extended. Grab the rings with both hands and walk your feet forward until your body is at an angle. - Pull your chest towards the rings by bending your elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together. Lower yourself back down with control. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your body straight from head to heels; avoid sagging your hips. - Engage your core throughout the movement to maintain stability. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - If rings are too challenging, you can perform inverted rows using a barbell or a sturdy table. - Alternatively, you can do banded rows with a resistance band. ## Loading and Rep-Modification Options - **Sweat:** - Row/Ski: 2:00 - Box Step-Ups: 16”/8” box - Kettlebell: 16kg/8kg - Ring Rows: 8 reps - **Train:** - Row/Ski: 3:00 - Box Step-Ups: 20”/12” box - Kettlebell: 20kg/12kg - Ring Rows: 10 reps - **Compete:** - Row/Ski: 3:00 - Box Step-Ups: 24”/16” box - Kettlebell: 24kg/16kg - Ring Rows: 12 reps ### Strategy Advice - Focus on maintaining a steady pace throughout the workout. It’s better to keep moving consistently than to sprint and burn out. - Break up the box step-ups and kettlebell high pulls into manageable sets if needed, especially if you start to feel fatigued. - Stay mindful of your form; quality over quantity is key today! Alright team, let’s get after it! Remember to hydrate, listen to your body, and have fun!
